Getting an image using web get command.¶

In [9]:
!wget 'https://vetmed.tamu.edu/news/wp-content/uploads/sites/9/2023/05/AdobeStock_472713009.jpeg'

Libraries used in image processing¶

  • matplotlib.image
  • pillow
  • openCV(cv2)
In [10]:
import matplotlib.image as mpimg
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

loding the image through matplotlib.image module¶

In [11]:
img = mpimg.imread("dog.jpeg")
In [12]:
type(img)
Out[12]:
numpy.ndarray
In [13]:
print(img.shape) 

(1080, 1440, 3)

img[0].shape
Out[14]:
(1440, 3)

(1440, 3)

(1440, 3)

How to convert numpy array to image?:¶

In [18]:
img_plot = plt.imshow(img)  # here img is numpy representation of the image
plt.show()
In [19]:
img.shape  # gives(height, width, channel number)
Out[19]:
(1080, 1440, 3)

Resize the image to a common dimension if we have many image:¶

  • Using Pillow Library
In [20]:
from PIL import Image
In [21]:
img = Image.open("dog.jpeg")
img_resized = img.resize((200,200))
In [22]:
# Save this resized image 
img_resized.save("Dog_image_Resized.jpg")
In [ ]:
In [23]:
# Read and display the resized image 
img_res = mpimg.imread("Dog_image_Resized.jpg")
plt.imshow(img_res)
plt.show()
In [24]:
# Chec the dimension 
print(img_res.shape)

(200, 200, 3)

How to convert RGB in to Gray Scale image:¶

  • using openCV
In [25]:
import cv2  # Computer vision
In [26]:
img = cv2.imread("dog.jpeg")
In [27]:
type(img)
Out[27]:
numpy.ndarray
In [28]:
img.shape
Out[28]:
(1080, 1440, 3)
In [29]:
plt.imshow(img)
Out[29]:
<matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x76ab09f169d0>
In [30]:
grayscale_image = cv2.cvtColor(img, cv2.COLOR_RGB2GRAY)
plt.imshow(grayscale_image)
Out[30]:
<matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x76ab08b1efd0>
In [31]:
grayscale_image.shape
Out[31]:
(1080, 1440)
In [32]:
#img.shape
In [33]:
plt.imshow(grayscale_image, cmap="gray")
plt.axis("off")
plt.show()
In [34]:
 # Saving the gray scale image :
cv2.imwrite("Dog_gray_scale_image.jpg", grayscale_image)
Out[34]:
True
In [35]:
grayscale_image.shape
Out[35]:
(1080, 1440)

Resize this resized image into 200*200 pixels¶

  • pillow is used for resizing.
In [36]:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.image as mpimg
from PIL import Image

# Open grayscale image
img_G = Image.open("Dog_gray_scale_image.jpg")
print(img_G.size)  # (width, height)

# Resize
img_G_resized = img_G.resize((200, 200))

# Save
img_G_resized.save("Resized_Gray_image.jpg")

# Load the saved image
Resized_Gray_image_display = mpimg.imread("Resized_Gray_image.jpg")

# Display correctly
plt.imshow(Resized_Gray_image_display, cmap="gray")
plt.axis("off")
plt.show()
img_G_resized.size

(1440, 1080)
Out[36]:
(200, 200)
In [37]:
import cv2
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.image as mpimg

# Read the image in grayscale
img_gray_ = cv2.imread("Dog_gray_scale_image.jpg", cv2.IMREAD_GRAYSCALE)
print(img_gray_.shape)

# Resize the grayscale image
resized_gray_ = cv2.resize(img_gray_, (200, 200))  # (width, height)
print(resized_gray_.shape)

# Save the image using OpenCV
cv2.imwrite("Resized_Gray_image_usingCV2.jpg", resized_gray_)

# Load the saved image
Resized_Gray_image_usingCV2_display_ = mpimg.imread("Resized_Gray_image_usingCV2.jpg")

# Display correctly
plt.imshow(Resized_Gray_image_usingCV2_display_, cmap="gray")
plt.axis("off")
plt.show()

(1080, 1440)
(200, 200)
